## Goals and Objectives (Stef)

The goal of this project is for PaintCare to launch with a fully functional redesigned Site Locator tool powered via a custom Wordpress plugin on their website. On the front end, the user interface should be mobile-first in design, able to grow with PaintCare’s needs, including support for future product categories beyond paint (such as aerosols in 2027/2028), and support Spanish translation.

On the back end, the Wordpress plugin should be able to sync seamlessly with the existing Filemaker database and be easy for content admins in Wordpress to make edits and additions to site locations.

There are two *backends*

- **WordPress backend (will be the focus for this project)**
- Offline FileMaker DB (no changes to be made to that DB as part of this project)

## Site Locator Features

1. What do you love about your current site locator map?

   - Individual site listings - the names of the stores are clear and bold text, easy to read on screen

   - Autofill of location when searching is good for the UX

   - Map zoom in/out function works well

   - When viewing the locator instance on each PaintCare state page, user first sees that state zoomed out with all flags - good for anyone that is searching via state page and wanting to focus on just their state

2. What if anything would you like us to make sure we keep?

   - Maintain sync with Filemaker database

   - Clear legible text

   - Autofill for searching

   - When visitor is on State page: should show the state the page is about - continue providing state page instances that start with a view of the full state and all flags in that state

   - Currently a label comes up within the plugin that tells users when they are viewing a non-PaintCare state - this is important and should be kept.

   - They are happy with the content displayed for the Sites but should display more above

3. What are the pain points?

   - For PaintCare team

     1. **Mobile experience is bad - hard to see the relevant info, the plugin also has not loaded for some users (unsure why)**

     2. The map legend information is practically invisible - users don’t know what the legend button symbols mean and likely do not click on them

     3. Edits and changes to locator are difficult, require highly specialized developer work

     4. The label that tells users when they are looking at a non-PaintCare state can only be turned on/off and adjusted by pro developers currently, would be nice if staff can turn that on/off

        ![](data:image/png;base64,)

     5. Tracking what / how the locator is being used is not possible

   - For Site Visitors? Have you heard any feedback about the use of this form

     1. Mobile experience is bad - hard to see the relevant info, the plugin also has not loaded for some users (unsure why)

     2. Difficult to search a neighboring state when you are zoomed in on one state - the map likes to focus and stick within state borders

     3. legend information is unknown or hard to find/understand

        ![](data:image/png;base64,)

     4. Navigating to another state is confusing – easy to miss the “Switch active state…” Also the “Switch active state…” does not translate

        ![](data:image/png;base64,)

     5. Mapbox was chosen because they use it internally for more detailed mapping requirements. Initially were concerned that it would not be as usable than Google Maps but seems to be working OK (= no need to necessarily change from Mapbox to another map)

     6. The box for the map takes up a large amount of the screen even when no map data is displayed due to the iFrame. We should intuitively adjust for this with better iFrame communication and optimize for the screen resolution of your device.

4. What would you like us to make sure we do NOT keep the same?

   - Landing page “splash” image for main locator instance on <https://www.paintcare.org/drop-off-sites/> - currently it’s an empty framed square with some text - it fills the space very awkwardly - would prefer a full map view such as users see when first landing in Google Maps

5. What would make this map easier to manage?

   - Easier updates and ability to make small changes (such as to text elements), staff ability to create new state page versions as new states join PaintCAre

   - The Limits per site is hard to see – make it more prominent in the Site data (perhaps display it in the initial listing view instead of behind “More information” . Discussed idea of it being one of the Filters and concern is limiting results too much but open to the idea (likely pushback from other stakeholders if the results are too limited by the Quantity) <https://cln.sh/Kf35ww10>

   - Note that there will be other products rolling out in the future that should appear under the dropdown.

   - **Content to display above the “More information/read more”**

     1. Location Name

     2. Address

     3. Phone

     4. Limit

     5. Accepted Products (and love use of imagery)

     6. *More Information*

   - Possibly More Filters

   - Key above the map (or more easily integrated)

   - Acronyms should not be used without clearly explaining in advance of seeing them (ex: HHW)

## Structure & Metrics

1. What do you like about the current content admin experience for the Site Locator? Can you walk us through what an update would look like right now if you were to add a site or edit details to a pre-existing site?
   1. We don’t currently access this. We have one colleague who manages adding/subtracting/editing sites on an offline Filemaker database. No changes are needed to that database - none have been requested.
2. What fields need to map to the FileMaker database? What does that mapping look like with the current Locator right now?
   1. For spanish translation - currently the locator
   2. I think pretty much all site listing fields come from the FM database and need to be mapped
3. What powers Spanish language translation on your website now
   1. Use of [Weglot Translation Plugin](https://www.weglot.com/) - which currently does not translate the front-end UI copy because everything within the current locator is passing to the WP site via a third party database
   2. We will use Weglot to translate these front-end elements (submit, information etc.)
4. What metrics would you like to be able to track? (Looker Studio)
   1. Zip/location entered
   2. Location Clicks

## Content and Design (Sebrinia)

1. Icons
   1. Open to change
   2. Icons do not need to appear on the map
2. Colors
   1. Open to change
3. Data Requirements
   1. Should all content/data that is currently available be included?
      1. All site listing content should remain the same, and it comes from the database sync. Text within the legend buttons needs to be edited/reduced and may depend or be impacted by the actual new design.
      2. Chelsea to check if the data can be separated (see screenshot below)
   2. Is there any additional data/information that needs to be added?
4. Review of Lo Fi Wireframe
   1. Mobile user flow for audience
5. Reference Sites:
   1. Site Locator <https://www.paintcare.org/drop-off-sites/>
   2. Inspirational Sites
   3. <https://www.productcare.org/recycling-locator/>
   4. [References of current product](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fo/o8637b1ipoz9to85asm4e/AA2vY2FfZMIaxh_pd5rkfqY?rlkey=c6a3zd3bvg2r4z55wextstakr&st=mca2z0mb&dl=0) (before screenshots and recordings, desktop & mobile)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

## Logistics

1. Filemaker access needed for [hosting+paintcare@4sitestudios.com](mailto:hosting+paintcare@4sitestudios.com)

   1. Do we need to coordinate with the previous Filemaker Dev? Patrick Evans, FileMaker Developer. [dbservices.com](http://www.dbservices.com/)

2. API Credentials that are provided to the AWS middleman gateway (this may need to be provided by IncaTech?)

   1. IncaTech made the original plugin (the original developer is no longer there).

3. Is there any documentation on the current map implementation?

   1. Task for Chelsea to request

## Process & Timeline

1. Are there any **upcoming events** that will affect the timeline, such as time off, staff retreats, annual meetings, or board meetings?

   1. 

2. What **staff** will be involved in the day-to-day project process? Please list names, titles and responsibilities.

   1. Who will be the primary contact?

      1. Chelsea

   2. Who is responsible for sign off on decisions?

      1. Chelsea and Brett

   3. Are there people not on this meeting that will be involved in the review and/or decision making process?

      1. PaintCare senior team (5 members including Brett)

3. [**Timeline**](https://app.productive.io/2650-4site-interactive-studios-inc/projects/768649)

   January 5-9

- 📆 4Site internal project kickoff

  January 12-23

- 📆 Paintcare/4Site project kickoff

- Draft design

  January 26-30

- 📆 Jan. 27: Draft design presentation (will schedule during kickoff)

  February 2-6

- PaintCare provides consolidated feedback on draft design

  February 9-13

- Feb. 10: 4Site revision 1 to design

- Feb. 13: PaintCare provides consolidated feedback on revision 1

  February 16-20

- Feb. 16: 4Site revision 2 to design

- Feb. 18: PaintCare provides consolidated (final) feedback on design (revision 2)

- Feb. 19: 4Site finalizes design for handoff to Dev

- **Feb. 20: Design approval**

- Feb. 20: Technical implementation plan is created

  February 23-March 20

- Development

  March 23-27

- 4Site QA and Improvements

  March 30-April 10

- April 1: 📆 Knowledge transfer meeting

- PaintCare conducts QA

  April 13-17

- 4Site Improvements from PaintCare QA

- **April 16: Launch Approval**

  April 20

- 🚀April 20: Launch!

  April 21-May 1

- Post launch support

### Meetings

1. Jan. 13: Kickoff
2. Jan. 27: Draft design review
3. April 1: Knowledge Transfer
